text: Before writing or posting a job, clarify which sourcing channel fits the role and candidate persona. Senior or niche roles often need LinkedIn, direct search, referral, or targeted communities. Junior roles and active candidate pools may respond well to job posts or Facebook groups when the content is specific and credible.
embedding_text:
Title: Job posting strategy: channel selection and candidate persona
Use when user asks how to choose sourcing channels for a role, LinkedIn vs Facebook vs job portals, senior vs junior roles, IT positions, direct search, referral, targeted communities, job posting strategy, sourcing channel, candidate persona.

metadata.customer_facing_summary: Guides employers on selecting optimal sourcing channels based on role type and candidate persona.
usable_principles: Match sourcing channels to candidate experience levels
Use candidate persona analysis to tailor job post content and platform selection
Use targeted groups or direct search for niche roles

text: Candidate persona is the profile of the candidate you are trying to attract. It should include level, years of experience, current workplace, motivations, pain points, preferred channels, reasons to apply, and reasons they may hesitate. This persona guides sourcing and messaging.
embedding_text:
Title: Candidate persona building and branding for sourcing
Use when user asks how to define candidate persona, what candidate information to gather, level, experience, pain points, channels, motivations, reasons to apply, reasons not to apply, sourcing message, hiring persona.

metadata.customer_facing_summary: Guides recruiters on building candidate personas for effective hiring.
usable_principles: Candidate personas should include career level, experience, pain points, and preferred communication channels
Use persona insights to tailor sourcing and messaging

text: Employer branding helps candidates decide whether a company feels trustworthy, growth-oriented, and worth their time. Candidates often look for trust, growth, and workplace happiness signals. Founder credibility, transparent policies, real achievements, and clear culture stories can improve trust.
embedding_text:
Title: Employer branding trust growth and candidate confidence
Use when user asks how to improve employer branding, build trust with candidates, explain company credibility, growth opportunity, fun workplace, startup founder profile, candidate confidence, apply decision.

metadata.customer_facing_summary: Provides employer branding strategies and candidate trust/growth factors.
usable_principles: Candidates evaluate companies based on trust, growth opportunities, and workplace happiness
Trust is built through credible leadership, transparent policies, and real proof points

text: Interview process depends on company and role. It may include one to five rounds, technical tests, presentations, face-to-face interviews, and candidate evaluation after interviews. The goal is to assess fit against the role, compare evidence, and prepare negotiation or offer decisions.
embedding_text:
Title: Interview process stages and evaluation to offer and onboarding
Use when user asks about interview process steps, screening, technical test, presentation, interview rounds, candidate evaluation, negotiation, offer, onboarding, structured hiring process.

metadata.customer_facing_summary: Structured hiring process framework with evaluation steps, negotiation, and onboarding protocols.
usable_principles: Customize interview rounds by role complexity
Use evidence from interviews before offer decisions

text: A compelling offer is a package, not only salary. It can include monetary and non-monetary benefits such as leave, growth opportunity, environment, manager quality, learning, and onboarding. Increasing salary alone may not solve candidate concerns if the real issue is growth, trust, or fit.
embedding_text:
Title: Offer package mindset and onboarding basics
Use when user asks about offer risk, compensation package, salary negotiation, monetary benefits, non-monetary benefits, onboarding, candidate acceptance, candidate concerns, growth, trust, fit.

metadata.customer_facing_summary: Provides strategies for compelling compensation packages and offer acceptance.
usable_principles: Compensation should include both monetary and non-monetary benefits
Offer risk can come from growth, trust, fit, and onboarding, not just salary

text: Job posts should avoid vague requirements. Instead of saying candidates need experience, specify must-have skills, years of experience, technology, and clear calls to action. Put attractive benefits and salary signals early when available, and separate must-have from nice-to-have requirements.
embedding_text:
Title: Job post optimization: must-have requirements and clear CTA
Use when user asks how to improve job posting, job post, JD clarity, must-have, nice-to-have, technical requirements, years of experience, CTA, benefits, salary, IT job post, attract suitable candidates.

metadata.customer_facing_summary: Provides actionable guidelines for optimizing job posts and technical requirement clarity.
usable_principles: Specify technical requirements with concrete examples
Prioritize benefits and salary in the first lines when available
Differentiate between must-have and nice-to-have requirements
